content.problemStatement:
Chef has three numbers A,B,A, B,A,B, and CCC.

He can do the following type of operation:


Select two numbers amongst A,B,A, B,A,B, and CCC;
Add 111 to the selected numbers;
Subtract 111 from the remaining number.
Determine whether Chef can make all the three numbers **equal** after applying the above operation any number of times.

If yes, output the **minimum** number of operations required by Chef.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of single line containing 333 space-separated integers A,B,A, B,A,B, and CCC.


Output Format
For each test case, output −1-1−1 if the numbers cannot be made equal, else output the **minimum** number of operations required to make them equal.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It can be proven that we cannot make the numbers equal using any number of operations.
Test case 222: We require a minimum of 222 operations to make the numbers equal:

Operation 111: Select the numbers AAA and CCC. Thus, AAA and CCC become 3+1=43+1 = 43+1=4 and 3+1=43+1 = 43+1=4 respectively. Also, BBB becomes 7−1=67-1 = 67−1=6.
Operation 222: Select the numbers AAA and CCC. Thus, AAA and CCC become 4+1=54+1 = 54+1=5 and 4+1=54+1 = 54+1=5 respectively. Also, CCC becomes 6−1=56-1 = 56−1=5.

Thus, all 333 numbers are equal after 222 operations.
Test case 333: Since all 333 numbers are already equal, we require no operations.
Test case 444: It can be proven that we cannot make the numbers equal using any number of operations.
